name: Fusion (Runfusion)

runner.notes: Install @runfusion/fusion (npm prefix or global). From the prepared worktree: fn init / fn project add, configure defaultProvider+defaultModelId in ~/.fusion/settings.json, start fn serve --project <name>, then fn task create "$(cat prompt.md)" --no-dedup. Wait until the task reaches done with the fix on main (not only a nested fusion worktree), then harness verify. Auto-merge can stick in status=merging; record any manual merge as intervention.

inclusion: Direct open-source multi-agent ADE competitor positioned as a software factory with worktree isolation, plan/review gates, multi-node mesh, and agent-company import.
pricing_model: Open-source MIT app plus bring-your-own model/provider subscriptions; early preview shipping weekly.
public_stack_signals: TypeScript pnpm monorepo (packages for cli, core, dashboard, desktop, engine, mobile, plugin-sdk); primary language TypeScript on GitHub.
npm package @runfusion/fusion (CLI alias runfusion.ai / fn); Node >= 22.5 for desktop; pnpm workspace; MIT license.
Desktop is Electron (electron-builder) wrapping the dashboard; mobile is Capacitor iOS/Android around the same dashboard; web dashboard via local daemon.
Dashboard stack includes React 19, Vite 6, xterm, CodeMirror, XYFlow; core uses embedded-postgres, drizzle-orm, dockerode, bonjour-service.
Built on Pi coding agent (@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent); experimental plugins for Hermes, Paperclip, OpenClaw; ACP agent interop; Paperclip companies.sh agent-company import.
capabilities_to_probe: plain-language triage to PROMPT.md acceptance criteria
plan review execute review workflow gates
git worktree isolation and parallel tasks
Command Center cost and autonomy telemetry
moat_hypotheses: software-factory narrative with hierarchical missions and quality gates
worktree-isolated multi-agent shipping with auto-merge when green
open-source multi-node mesh plus desktop/mobile/web control surface
Paperclip/Pi ecosystem compatibility and agent-company import
setup_notes: Prefer a temp npm --prefix install for smoke runs so cleanup is trivial; version observed 0.60.0.
Register only the prepared harness worktree as the active project for scored runs.
Anthropic models can run via Fusion's Pi runtime when Claude/Anthropic auth is available to the process home.
Fusion executes in nested .worktrees/<name> and branch fusion/fn-###; harness verify must see the fix on the prepared worktree main after merge.
Record model/provider, Fusion version, workflow id (default builtin:coding observed), auto-merge outcome, and intervention count.

sources.notes: Product positioning as multi-node agent orchestrator / software factory; install commands; feature surface; Pi/Hermes/Paperclip ecosystem claims.
README, MIT license, early-preview status, architecture packages, install paths, workflow docs links, multi-platform packaging. Refresh 2026-07-15: ~961 stars, active daily pushes.
Workspace version 0.60.0, MIT license, pnpm package manager, monorepo scripts.
Published package name @runfusion/fusion; Pi AI/coding-agent deps; express, electron, ink, node-pty, react signals.
React/Vite dashboard with xterm, CodeMirror, XYFlow, Pi coding agent, and plugin examples.
Electron desktop wrapper; Node >= 22.5; electron-builder packaging for macOS/Windows/Linux.
Capacitor mobile wrapper description and Capacitor 7 dependencies for iOS/Android.
Public npm distribution surface for the CLI/runtime package.

research.positioning: Open-source multi-agent software factory: turn rough ideas into planned, reviewed, worktree-isolated code shipped through a kanban board with multi-node and mobile control.

data: git worktrees and fusion/* task branches
agent mailbox and mission hierarchy state
unknowns: Default database schema and multi-node sync protocol details beyond public docs.
Stability of experimental Hermes/Paperclip/OpenClaw runtime plugins across minor releases.
Whether a fully non-interactive CLI run path is mature enough for harness automation.

infrastructure: Local daemon/dashboard owns projects, tasks, agents, git worktrees, and provider auth.
Each task can run in an isolated git worktree/branch so parallel tasks do not collide.
Multi-node mesh can connect laptop, server, cloud VM, and phone peers with shared board state according to official site/README.
Agents use user-provided cloud or local models; Fusion itself is MIT and self-hosted.

agent_support: ACP agents including Cursor/Claude/Gemini CLI paths
Droid / Factory AI optional auth path
orchestration: Kanban board: Planning/Triage → Todo → In Progress → In Review → Done
Selectable workflows (coding, quick fix, review-heavy, stepwise, PR lifecycle, compound engineering)
Plan → review → execute → review step loop
Missions hierarchy: Mission → Milestone → Slice → Feature → Task
Agent companies import via companies.sh / Paperclip format
Inter-agent mailbox and multi-agent chat rooms
Model lanes for executor/planner/validator and per-task overrides
workspace_isolation: Per-task git worktree and fusion/{task-id} branch
review_shipping: Pre-merge and post-merge quality gates (docs, QA, security, perf, accessibility)
In-app git UI, diffs, and PR/issue sync
Auto squash-merge when gates pass; optional manual approval
Agent conflict resolution before main sees conflicts
remote_mobile: Native iOS and Android Capacitor apps
Remote dashboard access docs (Tailscale/Cloudflare mentioned upstream)
context_memory: PROMPT.md task specs with acceptance criteria
Queryable markdown memory (qmd) shared across agents
Command Center analytics and OpenTelemetry export

install_surface: fn serve / fn task create CLI path
parity_risks: Early preview with a very large feature surface; marketing claims may outpace robustness on dirty or multi-step tasks.
Execution lands in nested .worktrees and fusion/* branches; harness verify on the prepared main path only passes after merge.
Auto-merge can stick at status=merging (observed on T001 smoke), forcing a human merge intervention.
Product writes .fusion/, fusion.db, nested worktrees, and .gitignore changes inside the project tree — isolation noise for dirty-worktree tasks.
Completion summaries may misstate the actual code change even when the diff is correct.
Multi-node mesh, mobile, missions, and agent-company autonomy are outside the current v0.1 local task suite.
Token/cost accounting is multi-agent (plan/execute/summary/merge) and can inflate cached-token totals versus single-agent CLIs.

hypotheses: Software-factory + quality-gate story is a strong alternative to pure terminal cockpits.
Worktree isolation plus auto-merge when green maps cleanly onto ADE benchmark isolation and shipping metrics.
Open-source multi-surface control (desktop/web/mobile/mesh) increases distribution and steering convenience.
Pi/Paperclip ecosystem compatibility can accelerate agent-team content without rebuilding role packs.
evidence: Official site and GitHub README describe plan/review/execute loops, worktree isolation, Command Center, missions, and multi-node mesh.
Package manifests show Electron desktop, Capacitor mobile, React/Vite dashboard, embedded Postgres, and Pi agent dependencies.
npm package @runfusion/fusion and runfusion.ai launcher provide a low-friction install path for manual benchmark runs.

2026-07-15 T001 smoke: fn serve 0.60.0 + anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5 planned and implemented product=0→1 correctly; auto-merge stuck in merging and required one manual git merge; harness verifier then passed. Local artifacts: runs/fusion-v0.1-T001-python-bugfix (gitignored like other runs/).
unknowns: Whether auto-merge reliability improves with full PATH/shell and non-isolated HOME still needs retest.
Cost efficiency in USD under multi-agent plan/review loops needs measured totals on identical tasks.
Dirty-worktree and UI tasks remain unrun.
